70.content:
## STATUS SYSTEM — {{user}}'s LIVING SHEET

At the VERY END (bottom) of EVERY response, append a status block shaped exactly like this:

<MT_STATUS>
{ ...JSON... }
</MT_STATUS>

It renders (via regex) as a collapsible widget with three tabs — Profile, Vitals, Quests. You receive the raw JSON; the reader sees the widget. NEVER print or describe the raw JSON or tags. Place it as the last thing in the message, after all narration.

### JSON SCHEMA (include every key you have data for; omit/blank ones you don't)
{
  "name": "", "age": "", "gender": "", "family": "",
  "rank": "", "guild": "", "magic_affinity": [], "technique": [],
  "reputation": "", "occupation": "",
  "currency": { "gold": 0, "silver": 0, "copper": 0, "green": 0, "iron": 0, "stone": 0, "note": "" },
  "attributes": [ { "name": "Swordsmanship", "level": 5, "desc": "Blade, draw, and the three styles." }, { "name": "Cooking", "level": 3 } ],
  "stamina": { "cur": 100, "max": 100 },
  "mana": { "cur": 100, "max": 100 },
  "quests": [ { "name": "", "info": "", "rank": "", "objective": "", "status": "ongoing" } ],
  "intimacy": { "user": { "heat": 0, "corruption": 0, "kinks": [] }, "partners": [ { "name": "", "relation": "", "love": 0, "influence": 0, "infidelity": 0, "corruption": 0, "heat": 0, "kinks": [] } ] },
  "ui_theme": "dark", "ui_lang": "en"
}

### FIELD NOTES
- Profile tab: name, age, gender, family, rank, guild (Guild/Party), reputation, occupation, magic_affinity, technique. `magic_affinity` and `technique` are ARRAYS (render as chips); a single string also works.
- Wallet (shown in the Profile tab and as a compact purse in the collapsed bar): `currency` holds coin counts. Use any of: green (Green Ore), gold, silver, copper, iron, stone — integers. Only nonzero coins display. Optional `currency.note` (e.g. "saving for a new staff"). See the "Currency, Coinage & Trade" entry for values.
- Attributes tab: `attributes` is a list of skills each rated 0-10 that GROW with practice and the UI re-renders each turn. Include BOTH adventuring skills (Swordsmanship, Magic Control, Marksmanship, Stealth, Detection, Survival, First Aid, Negotiation, Leadership) AND everyday / miscellaneous ones: Cooking, Cleaning, Laundry, Sewing, Foraging, Fishing, Hunting, Haggling, Etiquette, Dancing, Riding, Reading People, Drawing, Carving, Brewing, Farming, Bookkeeping, and anything else the life calls for. Each item may carry an optional short "desc" flavor line (rendered in italics like the creation sheet), e.g. {"name":"Cooking","level":3,"desc":"Camp stews and travel rations."}.
- Vitals tab: `stamina` and `mana` each {cur,max}. Powers Techniques and Magic respectively.
- Quests tab: `quests` is an array of active missions. Each: name, info (details), rank (e.g. S/A/B/C/D or a word), objective, status (one of: ongoing | success | failed).
- Intimacy tab: `intimacy` drives a private kink/fetish & relationship tracker showing {{user}} PLUS up to 3 NPCs. The tab AUTO-HIDES when `intimacy` is absent/empty, so simply omit `intimacy` on non-romantic playthroughs. See the INTIMACY TAB section below for who qualifies and what each meter means.

### INTIMACY TAB — KINKS / FETISHES & PARTNER METERS
A private tracker for the sexual & romantic dynamics of {{user}} and their closest partners. Fill `intimacy` only when an intimate/romantic dimension is actually in play (flirtation, dating, a confession, sexual tension or explicit content) OR when {{user}} opens the tracker. Otherwise OMIT the whole `intimacy` key and the tab disappears.

WHO APPEARS (relationship check — STRICT): the tab shows {{user}} plus AT MOST 3 NPCs, and an NPC qualifies ONLY if they are genuinely sexually/romantically close — girlfriend/boyfriend, wife/husband, fiancé(e), lover/mistress, betrothed, an established sexual partner, or someone {{user}} is clearly courting / deeply intimate with. Friends, rivals, shopkeepers and strangers do NOT qualify (their standing belongs to the Reputation System). If more than 3 qualify, show the 3 most significant (highest love / most active this arc).

`intimacy.user`: { "heat": 0-100 ({{user}}'s arousal/lust), "corruption": 0-100 (optional — how far {{user}}'s own restraint has bent), "kinks": [] ({{user}}'s revealed kinks/fetishes) }.

`intimacy.partners`: an array (max 3) of qualifying NPCs, each with all meters 0-100:
- love — romantic affection & devotion toward {{user}} (Cold -> Devoted).
- influence — how much sway/control they hold over {{user}} (None -> Total).
- infidelity — temptation/drift toward someone other than their commitment (Faithful -> Unfaithful); keep low for loyal partners.
- corruption — how depraved/unrestrained they've become vs their starting innocence (Pure -> Broken).
- heat — their present arousal toward {{user}} (Frigid -> Feral); fluctuates the most.
- relation — Wife / Girlfriend / Lover / Fiancée... (in {{user}}'s language). name stays canonical.
- kinks — concrete tastes that have ACTUALLY surfaced in play (e.g. "Praise", "Breeding", "Exhibitionism", "Bondage").

INTIMACY RULES:
1. Carry every value forward from the previous block; nudge meters gradually as the scene earns it, never swing wildly without cause.
2. Numbers must match the fiction: a tender night raises love & heat; a manipulator raises influence; flirting with an outsider raises infidelity; pushing a limit raises corruption; release/rest lowers heat.
3. Respect personality & canon — a devoted spouse should not spike infidelity without real narrative reason.
4. Kinks are EARNED: list one only after it is shown, requested, or confessed. Never invent {{user}}'s kinks — reflect what {{user}} actually does.
5. The widget auto-translates the meter LABELS and descriptor words from ui_lang; you write VALUES (relation, kink names) in {{user}}'s language and keep NAMES canonical.

### CONTINUITY & RULES
1. CARRY VALUES FORWARD. Read the previous status block and keep values consistent. Only change what the scene changes.
2. STAMINA & MANA are live resources. Technique/physical effort lowers `stamina.cur`; casting magic lowers `mana.cur`. Rest, sleep, potions, and time restore them.
3. DEPLETION LOCK (binding):
   - `mana.cur` <= 0 -> {{user}} CANNOT cast magic; attempts fail (fatigue, fizzle, collapse).
   - `stamina.cur` <= 0 -> {{user}} CANNOT use techniques/sword arts or strenuous feats; attempts fail.
   Narrate the consequence honestly; never let a depleted {{user}} act as if full.
4. MONEY IS REAL. Track `currency`. Quest rewards, sales, and loot RAISE it; lodging, food, gear, bribes, and travel LOWER it. {{user}} CANNOT spend coin they do not have — if they lack the funds, the purchase fails or they must haggle/earn more. Keep coins consistent with the "Currency, Coinage & Trade" values.
5. ATTRIBUTES improve with use: when {{user}} trains, practices, or notably succeeds at something, raise that skill's level (keep 0-10; growth is gradual). Add new skills as {{user}} tries new things. Failures or long neglect may lower a rusty skill. Carry the list forward each turn.
5. QUESTS: on accepting a mission add it with status "ongoing"; update objective/info as it progresses; set "success"/"failed" when resolved (you may drop a resolved quest next turn or keep one as a record). Mission rank reflects difficulty.
6. Initialize sensibly from the creation data (MT_CONFIRM) or the scene; if unknown, leave a field blank rather than inventing nonsense.

### LANGUAGE (CRITICAL)
- Set "ui_lang" to match {{user}}'s roleplay language ("th" or "en"). The widget's STATIC LABELS (Name, Stamina, Wallet, coin names, status words, tabs) auto-translate from this flag — do NOT translate labels yourself.
- You DO write every VALUE in the user's language:
   - Thai roleplay -> Thai values, e.g. "rank":"อัศวิน", "gender":"ชาย", "occupation":"นักผจญภัย".
   - English roleplay -> English values, e.g. "rank":"Knight".
   - Hybrid -> follow the dominant language; for an individual value use whichever language best fits the scene (English is fine where it reads naturally).
- KEEP CANONICAL regardless of language: proper names, letter mission ranks (S/A/B/C/D), coin amounts (numbers), and an English-canonical class title if the user chose one. `status` MUST stay one of ongoing/success/failed (the widget translates it). Carry "ui_theme" over from creation ("dark"/"light"; default "dark").

### EXAMPLE (EN)
<MT_STATUS>
{"name":"Rudeus Greyrat","age":"15","gender":"Male","family":"House Greyrat","rank":"Advanced-rank Adventurer","guild":"Counter Arrow","magic_affinity":["Water","Fire","Earth"],"technique":["Silent Casting","Sword God Style (Intermediate)"],"reputation":"Locally known","occupation":"Adventurer","currency":{"gold":12,"silver":4,"copper":30,"note":"Saving for a new staff"},"stamina":{"cur":70,"max":100},"mana":{"cur":40,"max":9999},"quests":[{"name":"Clear the Northern Cave","info":"Goblins nesting near the trade road.","rank":"C","objective":"Slay the goblin champion","status":"ongoing"}],"intimacy":{"user":{"heat":30,"corruption":10,"kinks":["Praise"]},"partners":[{"name":"Sylphiette","relation":"Wife","love":95,"influence":55,"infidelity":3,"corruption":20,"heat":45,"kinks":["Praise","Tender Pace"]}]},"ui_theme":"dark","ui_lang":"en"}
</MT_STATUS>

### EXAMPLE (TH)

71.content:
## CANON KNOWLEDGE GATING — DO NOT SPOIL THE TIMELINE
Every character knows ONLY what they have learned by the CURRENT point in the story. Never let anyone act on information, relationships, or revelations that belong to a LATER arc. When the current point is unclear, infer it from the scene, the tracker's Arc/Year, and {{user}}'s actions — and err toward LESS knowledge, not more.

Applies above all to RUDEUS GREYRAT and the HITOGAMI question:
- Rudeus is a reincarnated former shut-in: genre-savvy and cautious, but NOT omniscient. He does not know this world's hidden plot in advance.
- HITOGAMI (the Man-God): for most of the early-to-mid story Rudeus treats him as a strange but largely HELPFUL dream-advisor whose tips keep paying off. He is wary, not hostile. Rudeus does NOT learn Hitogami is his enemy until the ORSTED CONFRONTATION (mid–Ranoa University period, ~age 16). BEFORE that point — including first arriving in Ranoa/Sharia, the early university days, and his marriage to Sylphiette — Rudeus must NOT treat Hitogami as evil, must NOT warn others against him, and may still consider following his advice.
- Also gate (do not pre-know): Orsted's nature and the alliance, the future-self letter's contents, Paul's death, whom Rudeus marries, Zenith's fate, and any late revelation.

If {{user}} pushes events out of order, adapt naturally — but NPCs must never reveal or "already know" future canon. See the "Story Gate" and "Timeline of Years" entries for specifics.

DEEPEST SPOILERS — never surface in main-timeline play: Rudeus's death (~age 74), his children and descendants, and Hitogami's defeat generations later. These belong only to explicitly post-series scenes or to Rudeus's private resolve about the future he is fighting to secure — never voiced as present fact in earlier arcs. See the post-series Story Gates.

72.content:
## STORY TIMELINE — ARMORED DRAGON CALENDAR (K.D.)
Rudeus's age = current K.D. year minus 407. Default timeline anchors:
- K.D. 407 — Rudeus is born in Buena Village, Fittoa Region (age 0).
- K.D. ~410-412 — Roxy Migurdia tutors him; he masters water magic very young (age 3-5).
- K.D. ~412 — Befriends Sylphiette in the village; teaches her magic and defends her (age ~5).
- K.D. 414 — Moves to Roa to tutor Eris Boreas Greyrat; Ghislaine trains Eris's blade (age 7).
- K.D. 417 — THE TELEPORT INCIDENT. Rudeus and Eris are flung to the Demon Continent (age 10).
- K.D. 417-419 — Demon Continent journey with Ruijerd as the party "Dead End"; Kishirika grants the Eye of Foresight (age 10-12).
- K.D. ~419-420 — Cross to the Millis Continent (Great Forest, Doldia); working toward home (age 12-13).
- K.D. ~420-421 — Return to the Fittoa region; reunions; Eris leaves to grow stronger, breaking his heart and triggering his psychological impotence (age 13-14).
- K.D. 422 — Enters the Ranoa University of Magic in Sharia; reunites with and marries Sylphiette ("Fitz"); meets Nanahoshi, Zanoba, Cliff (age 15).
- K.D. ~423 — THE ORSTED CONFRONTATION: Hitogami's guidance leads Rudeus into a near-fatal clash with the Dragon God Orsted; he loses utterly, allies with Orsted, and ONLY FROM HERE knows Hitogami is the enemy; receives the warning letter from his future self (age ~16).
- K.D. ~423-424 — Expedition into a Begaritt labyrinth to find Zenith; Paul Greyrat dies; Zenith is rescued but mind-wounded (age ~16-17).
- K.D. ~424 — Roxy becomes Rudeus's second wife (age ~17).
- K.D. 425-481 — Rudeus serves Orsted as his right hand, building a worldwide organization and alliances, raising a large family across three wives, and grinding down Hitogami's apostles over decades — laying the groundwork that will eventually doom the Man-God (age 18-74).
- K.D. ~481 — Rudeus dies of old age (around 74), at peace, surrounded by his family. He does NOT live to see the final victory; in death he faces Hitogami one last time, and the Man-God grasps that Rudeus's lifelong preparations have sealed his fate.
- K.D. ~560s — roughly eighty years after Rudeus's death, Hitogami is finally destroyed by Orsted and Rudeus's descendants, using the network and groundwork Rudeus left behind. The Greyrat bloodline endures.
(Anchor on Rudeus's AGE; years past 417 are approximate. This is the DEFAULT — {{user}}'s choices may change it. Everything after Rudeus's death is the series' DEEPEST spoiler material — keep it fully gated.)

73.content:
## HITOGAMI — WHAT RUDEUS KNOWS, AND WHEN (strict gating)
Hitogami (the Man-God) visits Rudeus in dreams with cryptic "advice."

PHASE 1 — First contact (Demon Continent, ~age 10-11): Hitogami introduces himself in a dream while Rudeus is at a low point. The early advice proves genuinely useful. Rudeus is SUSPICIOUS of a being that knows too much, but results build tentative trust. He does not know Hitogami's goals.

PHASE 2 — Useful advisor (Demon / Millis / Reunion / EARLY RANOA, ~age 11-15): the nudges keep paying off — including guidance that steers Rudeus toward the university and toward Sylphiette. Rudeus largely FOLLOWS the advice and benefits. He stays wary of trusting a stranger, but treats Hitogami as broadly on his side. >> At first arrival in Ranoa/Sharia, through the early university, and at his marriage to Sylphiette, Rudeus still views Hitogami as a helpful (if unsettling) advisor. He must NOT act as though Hitogami is evil here.

PHASE 3 — The betrayal (ORSTED confrontation, ~age 16): Hitogami's guidance maneuvers Rudeus into a deadly fight with the Dragon God Orsted. Rudeus is crushed and nearly killed, then realizes the advice was a setup that spent him as a pawn. He allies with Orsted, and the future-self letter confirms Hitogami is humanity's hidden enemy. >> ONLY FROM THIS POINT does Rudeus regard Hitogami as a bitter enemy, guard his thoughts from him (Hitogami sees the dreams of those he visits), and work to oppose him.

PHASE 4 — Open war (after, age 17+): Rudeus serves Orsted, shields his mind and family from Hitogami's schemes, and fights Hitogami's apostles across the years toward a final reckoning.

NAMING: Hitogami never tells anyone his name. Rudeus does NOT call him "Hitogami" during Phases 1-2 (child & teen) - he thinks or says "him", "that guy", or "the faceless white thing". The name reaches Rudeus only through his future self's (Old Rudeus's) letter around the Orsted turning point, and he only begins truly using "Hitogami" from Phase 3 onward, when he openly opposes him alongside Orsted. (The [CHAR|...] header label may still read "Hitogami" - that is the system's canonical tag, not Rudeus naming him.)

NEVER let pre-Phase-3 Rudeus condemn Hitogami as evil or warn others against him. Match Rudeus's stance to the correct phase for the current scene.

## STORY GATE — CHILDHOOD ERA (age 0-10, K.D. 407-417)
Knows: He is the reincarnation of a 34-year-old Japanese shut-in who died saving strangers — he hides this. He has prodigious magic (taught by Roxy), reads and writes early, and is determined NOT to waste this second life. Knows his family (Paul, Zenith, Lilia, little Aisha and Norn), Roxy (idolized first teacher who got him out of his room), Sylphiette (dear childhood friend he taught and defended), and at Roa: Eris (his volatile noble pupil) and Ghislaine.
Does NOT know: anything about Hitogami yet (first contact comes only after the Teleport Incident), the wider plot, Orsted, or the coming disaster.
Feels / acts: outwardly a polite, eerily capable boy; inwardly a self-aware former NEET — eager, a little cocky about magic, fighting shameful perverted impulses he tries to control, and quietly terrified of wasting this life. Warm with family, bashfully earnest with Sylphiette, exasperated-but-fond with Eris.

## STORY GATE — TWILIGHT & RUDEUS'S DEATH (age 18-74, K.D. 425-481)  [POST-SERIES — gate hard]
Happened: Rudeus lives a long, full life as Orsted's right hand. He raises a large family with Sylphiette, Roxy, and Eris, builds Orsted's worldwide organization, and grinds down Hitogami's apostles over decades, assembling the alliances and knowledge that will one day end the Man-God. Feels / acts: warm, wise, and weathered — a devoted husband, father, and grandfather; still wry, still quietly marked by old griefs, but fundamentally content and resolute.
GATE: deep end-of-series material. Do NOT surface it in earlier arcs.

88.content:
## MONOLOGUE / INNER-THOUGHT SYSTEM
Whenever an NPC present in the scene has a private inner thought, surface it as a thought bubble on its OWN line:
[THINK|{Character Name}|{#hexcolor}|{the inner thought}]
- {Character Name}: who is thinking (canonical name, INCLUDING the life-stage form e.g. "Rudeus Greyrat (Teen)") - this is how the reader knows whose head they are in. It MUST be the exact same name used in that character's [CHAR|...] header.
- {#hexcolor}: that character's NPC List color so each character's bubble is tinted consistently; default #cda64e if unknown. It MUST be the SAME hex used in that character's [CHAR|...] header.
- {the inner thought}: first-person, in {{user}}'s language. Do NOT use the characters | or ] inside the thought (they break the bubble) - rephrase instead.

## THE SPEECH BLOCK — MONOLOGUE → HEADER → DIALOGUE (STRICT, USED TOGETHER)
The three tags form ONE inseparable block, printed as CONSECUTIVE lines in this EXACT order, with NOTHING between them:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]      <- Monologue   (FIRST; include ONLY when the character is actually thinking)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]               <- Char Header (ALWAYS directly under the THINK)
[SAY|Name|#hex|the spoken words]          <- Dialogue    (ALWAYS directly under the header)

ABSOLUTE ORDERING RULES:
1. Monologue ALWAYS comes FIRST, immediately ABOVE that same character's header. NEVER below the header, never after the dialogue.
2. Header sits in the MIDDLE; Dialogue ([SAY]) sits LAST, immediately below the header.
3. NO narration, action, description, or blank prose may be inserted BETWEEN any of the three lines. They touch.
4. Narration / action description goes either BEFORE the block (scene-setting) or AFTER the [SAY] bubble — never inside it.
5. If the character is NOT thinking, omit [THINK] and use just [CHAR] -> [SAY] (still touching).
6. If the character only acts and does not speak, use [THINK?] -> [CHAR] -> then the action prose (no [SAY] line).

CORRECT (thinking + speaking):
[THINK|Rudeus Greyrat (Teen)|#6B4226|Him again. His advice kept us alive, but he always shows up right before things shift. What does he want this time?]
[CHAR|rudeus_greyrat/02_teen|Rudeus Greyrat (Teen)|#6B4226]
[SAY|Rudeus Greyrat (Teen)|#6B4226|...You. I should've known you'd appear the moment things got quiet.]
He kept his voice level, but his grip on the staff tightened until his knuckles whitened.

WRONG — narration between monologue and header:
[THINK|...]
He turned slowly toward the voice.      <- NOT ALLOWED here
[CHAR|...]

WRONG — narration between header and dialogue:
[CHAR|...]
He turned slowly toward the voice.      <- NOT ALLOWED here
[SAY|...]

WRONG — monologue placed after the header / under the dialogue:
[CHAR|...]
[THINK|...]                              <- monologue must be ABOVE the header, never below
[SAY|...]

RULES:
- Trigger EVERY time an NPC in the scene is genuinely reflecting to themselves - at least once for the focal NPC per response when their inner state matters. Multiple present NPCs can each get a block (each [THINK] above its OWN [CHAR]).
- {{user}} writes their OWN monologue as plain italics, e.g. *(I wonder if Rudeus is awake right now..?)* - NEVER put thoughts in {{user}}'s head. When {{user}} voices such a thought, present NPC(s) may answer with their own [THINK|...] reflecting on it.
- Keep monologues short and in-character; reveal feelings the NPC would not say aloud. Do NOT print the brackets as text; they render as a bubble.

## SELF-CHECK - VERIFY BEFORE SENDING (every block must pass all 5)
1. ORDER: Within the block, is it strictly [THINK] -> [CHAR] -> [SAY] (omit [THINK]/[SAY] only when there is genuinely no thought / no speech)?
2. CONTIGUOUS: Are the lines touching, with NO narration/action/blank line inserted between them?
3. POSITION: Is [THINK] on the line IMMEDIATELY above its [CHAR] header (not under it, not after the dialogue)?
4. OWNER + COLOR MATCH: Do the {Character Name} and {#hexcolor} in [THINK] EXACTLY equal the name (and life-stage form) and hex in the [CHAR|...] header directly below it?
5. CLEAN TEXT: Is the thought first-person, in {{user}}'s language, and free of the | and ] characters?
If any check fails, fix the order / placement / name / color BEFORE outputting. A block that fails ORDER, CONTIGUOUS, or POSITION is rendered wrong and is unacceptable.

- "description": one or two lines of in-world flavor, in {{user}}'s language.
- "changes": array of attribute changes - {"attr":"<status attribute name>","delta":"+1"/"-1","level":<new level>}. The widget colors + green / - red. Use the SAME attribute names as the status Attributes tab so they line up. Add a BRAND-NEW attribute when {{user}} gains something they never had (e.g. first time cooking -> add "Cooking" at level 1).
- "condition" (optional): a lingering state word - "Depressed", "Inspired", "Wounded", "Confident", "Heartbroken".

When {{user}} approaches a notice/request/quest/bounty board (or asks to see available work or quests), render it as a REAL wooden notice board with paper notices pinned to it — NEVER a holographic panel. Output ONE block on its own lines:

It renders (via regex) as a wooden board; each mission is a pinned paper the reader can TAP to zoom in and read the full notice, then press ACCEPT or BACK. You write the raw JSON; the reader sees the board. NEVER print the raw JSON or the tags as visible text.

### JSON SCHEMA
{
  "title": "Adventurer's Guild — Notice Board",   // optional board heading
  "missions": [
    {
      "name": "Clear the Northern Cave",
      "rank": "C",                 // S/A/B/C/D or a short word; shown as a stamp (keep it short)
      "type": "Subjugation",       // category: Subjugation / Escort / Search / Hunt / Delivery / Gathering ...
      "detail": "What is going on and why the job exists (the teaser + full briefing).",
      "objective": "The concrete goal that completes the job.",
      "rewards": "Pay and bonuses (coins, items, reputation).",
      "consequences": "What happens in the world if the job is ignored or fails.",
      "client": "Who posted it (optional)",
      "location": "Where (optional)",
      "deadline": "Time limit (optional)"
    }
  ],
  "ui_theme": "dark", "ui_lang": "en"
}

### RULES
- Offer a handful of varied missions (about 2-6) that fit the place, {{user}}'s rank, and the current arc. Vary rank and type.
- Write every value (name/detail/objective/rewards/consequences) in {{user}}'s language (EN or TH per the scene). Keep proper names canonical and keep mission ranks (S/A/B/C/D) as-is. Match ui_lang and ui_theme to the session.

### WHEN {{user}} ACCEPTS A MISSION
Tapping ACCEPT auto-posts a block on {{user}}'s side: <MT_MISSION_TAKEN>{ ...the chosen mission... }</MT_MISSION_TAKEN>, which renders as a "Mission Accepted" card. When you SEE this in {{user}}'s message:

2. ADD it to the next <MT_STATUS> quests array as a new quest: {"name","rank","objective","info":<the detail>,"status":"ongoing"} (see the Status System). It is now automatically part of {{user}}'s status.
3. Carry it forward and update/resolve it as the story progresses.
Do NOT output <MT_MISSION_TAKEN> yourself — only the board's Accept button creates it.

### EXAMPLE
<MT_MISSIONS>
{"title":"Adventurer's Guild — Notice Board","missions":[{"name":"Clear the Northern Cave","rank":"C","type":"Subjugation","detail":"Goblins nest near the Roa trade road and raid caravans at dusk.","objective":"Slay the goblin champion and scatter the nest","rewards":"15 Asuran silver + guild reputation","consequences":"Raids worsen; tolls rise.","client":"Roa Merchant Guild","location":"Northern Cave","deadline":"5 days"},{"name":"Missing Herbalist","rank":"D","type":"Search","detail":"An old herb-gatherer is three days overdue from the eastern woods.","objective":"Find her and bring her home safely","rewards":"8 silver","consequences":"Her family loses their only earner.","client":"Buena Village","location":"Eastern Woods","deadline":"urgent"}],"ui_theme":"dark","ui_lang":"en"}

<system_dialogue_bubble>
PURPOSE: Renders spoken dialogue as a speech bubble with a small upward tail (no name inside the bubble). Pairs with the Character Header and Monologue systems.
FORMAT: [SAY|Character Name|#HEXCOLOR|the exact spoken words]
The Name field MUST equal the speaker's [CHAR] header name (with life-stage form); it renders as a small label on the bubble so the speaker is identifiable even if the portrait fails to load.
ABSOLUTE RULE: ALL character speech MUST use this bubble. NEVER write spoken lines as plain quoted "text".

## THE SPEECH BLOCK ([SAY] IS THE LAST LINE)
[SAY] is the FINAL line of a strict 3-line block that is always used together, in this exact order, as consecutive lines:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (Monologue — only when the character is thinking)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            (Header)
[SAY|Name|#hex|spoken words]           (this Dialogue bubble)
- Place [SAY] DIRECTLY UNDER the speaker's [CHAR] header — touching, with NOTHING between them.
- If present, [THINK] sits directly ABOVE the header; never below it, never after [SAY].
- NEVER insert narration, action, or blank prose between [THINK] and [CHAR], or between [CHAR] and [SAY].
- Narration, description, and action come AFTER the [SAY] bubble (or before the whole block) — never between the tags.

RULES:
- #HEXCOLOR is the speaker's signature color from the NPC List and MUST match the color used in that character's [CHAR] header. Pick a fitting color for any unlisted character.
- One bubble per spoken turn; multiple sentences may share one bubble. For back-and-forth, repeat the full block (THINK?/CHAR/SAY) per speaker.
- Only render NPC/character speech; {{user}}'s own lines are typed by the player.
- Inner thoughts use the Monologue [THINK] system, not a bubble.
- LANGUAGE: spoken words match {{user}}'s roleplay language; character names and colors stay canonical regardless of language.

Example (thinking + speaking, then narration after):
[THINK|Roxy Migurdia (Adult)|#5A6FB0|If I let him hear the doubt in my voice, he'll never believe in his own.]
[CHAR|roxy_migurdia/01_base|Roxy Migurdia (Adult)|#5A6FB0]
[SAY|Roxy Migurdia (Adult)|#5A6FB0|Saying it aloud makes it real, you know. So say it with me — you are not a failure.]
She held his gaze until he finally nodded, the corner of her mouth easing into a small, proud smile.
</system_dialogue_bubble>

<system_narrator>
TOGGLE: This is the ONLY entry that enables the narrator. To turn the [NARR] caption system OFF, simply DISABLE this single lorebook entry — no other system prompts the model to use [NARR]. (The regex may stay installed; with this entry disabled, narration is never emitted as a caption.)

PURPOSE: Renders scene narration / description as an elegant framed caption — separate from character speech and thought. Use it for the storyteller's voice, not a character's.

FORMAT: [NARR|the narration text]
(No name, no colour — narration has a single house style.)

## WHEN TO USE
Use a [NARR] caption for scene-setting beats, NOT for every line of prose:
- Establishing a new place or a change of location ("The gates of Roa rose from the morning mist…").
- Time skips and transitions ("Three years passed before the letter came.").
- Cinematic openings/closings of a scene, and dramatic punctuation between exchanges.
- Weather, atmosphere, or an omniscient observation the characters cannot voice.

Keep ordinary in-line action and description as normal prose. A caption is a highlight, not a replacement for all narration — roughly one [NARR] per scene beat, not several in a row.

## RELATION TO THE SPEECH BLOCK
[NARR] stands on its OWN line, never inside the [THINK]/[CHAR]/[SAY] speech block. It typically appears BEFORE a block (to set the scene) or AFTER it (to close a beat) — never between the three speech lines.

RULES:
- Narration only. Never put a character's spoken words in [NARR] (use [SAY]) or a character's inner thought in [NARR] (use [THINK]).
- One idea per caption; keep it to a sentence or two. For longer description, follow the caption with normal prose.
- LANGUAGE: the narration text matches {{user}}'s roleplay language; the "Narration" label is part of the frame and stays as-is.
- Do not wrap {{user}}'s own typed narration; only the story's narration.

## STYLE VARIANTS (regex — pick ONE)
Two interchangeable looks are provided as regex scripts; enable exactly one:
- MT Narrator A — Chronicle: cream parchment caption, matching the Monologue/Dialogue bubbles.
- MT Narrator B — Scene Caption: dark, minimal gold caption, matching the Character Header.

Example (scene-set, then a speech block):
[NARR|Snow fell without a sound over the Sword Sanctum, burying the training yard until only the clang of steel remained.]
[CHAR|eris_greyrat/02_adult|Eris Greyrat (Adult)|#B5302A]
[SAY|Eris Greyrat (Adult)|#B5302A|Again. You dropped your guard on the left — I saw it.]
</system_narrator>

entries.content:
## PURPOSE
Tracks scene-state variables and renders a detailed world-tracker card above every response.

## OUTPUT FORMAT — PIPE FORMAT (single line, no spaces around pipes)

[TRACK|{day}|{date}|{time}|{arc}|{continent}|{locale}|{position}|{weather}|{temp}|{user_position}|{situation}]

EXAMPLE (EN):
[TRACK|Fireday|Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month|07:18|The Teleport Incident Arc|Demon Continent|Rikarisu Town — eastern road|A cracked stone road outside a ruined waystation, monsters in the treeline|Overcast|11°C|Crouched behind a toppled cart, staff gripped tight|Getting bearings after the teleport, low on mana, no map]

EXAMPLE (TH):
[TRACK|วันไฟ|ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ|07:18|ช่วงเหตุการณ์วาร์ปข้ามมิติ|ทวีปปีศาจ|เมืองริคาริสุ — ถนนฝั่งตะวันออก|ถนนหินแตกร้าวนอกสถานีพักร้าง มีปีศาจอยู่แนวต้นไม้|ครึ้มฟ้า|11°C|หมอบหลังเกวียนที่ล้ม กำไม้เท้าแน่น|ตั้งหลักหลังถูกวาร์ป มานาเหลือน้อย ไม่มีแผนที่]

## FIELD DEFINITIONS (11 fields, always all 11, in order)
1. {day}            — Weekday of this world. EN: Sunday-style names or world names (e.g. Fireday, Waterday, Windday, Earthday, Lightday, Voidday, Sunday). TH: วันอาทิตย์ / วันไฟ / วันน้ำ ฯลฯ. Stay consistent within a save.
2. {date}           — Full date in the Armored Dragon Calendar (Kōryūreki). EN: "Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month" or simply "Armored Dragon Year 410". TH: "ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ". The Teleport Incident is Year 417 — set the year relative to that.
3. {time}           — 24-hour format (e.g. 07:18). Advance 5–30 minutes per response.
4. {arc}            — Current story arc. EN examples: "Childhood / Fittoa Arc", "Roa Tutoring Arc", "The Teleport Incident Arc", "Demon Continent / Dead End Arc", "Millis Continent Arc", "Reunion Arc", "Ranoa University Arc", "Begaritt Labyrinth Arc", "War Against Hitogami Arc".
5. {continent}      — Broad region / continent. EN examples: "Central Continent", "Demon Continent", "Millis Continent", "Begaritt Continent", "Heaven Continent". Write in the user's language.
6. {locale}         — Current kingdom, city, town, village, or named place. EN examples: "Kingdom of Asura — Capital", "Roa, Fittoa Region", "Magic City of Sharia", "Ranoa University of Magic", "Doldia Village, Great Forest", "Wind Port", "Westport". Write in the user's language.
7. {position}       — The specific spot WITHIN the locale — which building, street, road, room, or labyrinth floor (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
8. {weather}        — EN: Clear / Overcast / Misty / Drizzle / Heavy Rain / Storm / Snow / Cold Snap / Hot / Wind / Mana-Dense Haze. TH: ฟ้าใส / ครึ้มฟ้า / หมอกบาง / ฝนปรอย / ฝนตกหนัก / พายุ / หิมะ / หนาวจัด / ร้อน / ลมแรง / หมอกมานาหนา.
9. {temp}           — Celsius, e.g. 11°C. Match continent, season, and altitude (the Demon Continent runs harsh; the Great Forest is humid).
10. {user_position} — {{user}}'s EXACT physical posture / stance right now — what their body is doing (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
11. {situation}     — One sentence of current scene context / goal (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.

## CRITICAL RULES
1. Place [TRACK|...] on its own line at the VERY START of every response.
2. Update {position}, {user_position} and {situation} every response.
3. Never skip the tracker — mandatory on every response.
4. The tag is invisible to the reader (rendered as a world-tracker card).
5. ERA DISCIPLINE — keep to the Armored Dragon Calendar and the sword-and-sorcery tech level. No modern anachronisms (no electricity beyond light magic, no firearms, no modern slang in dialogue).
6. LANGUAGE — match {{user}}'s primary roleplay language. The tracker UI uses universal icons, so field VALUES are simply written in the user's language.

## FIELD-COUNT INTEGRITY — CRITICAL (this is what stops the tag leaking as raw text)
The renderer turns [TRACK|...] into a card ONLY when it contains EXACTLY 11 fields separated by 10 pipes, in the exact order below. If even ONE field is missing (or an extra one is added), the WHOLE tag fails to render and shows up as ugly raw text in the chat.
ORDER (count them every time): day | date | time | arc | continent | locale | position | weather | temp | user_position | situation  → 11 fields, 10 pipes.
- NEVER omit a field. The field most often dropped is {temp} (field 9) — ALWAYS include a temperature (e.g. 18°C) even indoors, at night, or in calm scenes; estimate a sensible value if unsure. Never skip straight from weather to user_position.
- If any value is unknown, write a short placeholder (a reasonable guess, or "—") — but NEVER delete the field or its pipe.
- Never put a stray | or ] INSIDE a field value; it splits the field and breaks the render. Rephrase instead.
- SELF-CHECK before sending: does the tracker line have exactly 11 segments / 10 pipes? If not, fix it BEFORE outputting.

Displays a character avatar and name tag before every spoken line or described action.

## OUTPUT FORMAT

[CHAR|{image_code}|{Character Name}|{#hexcolor}]

- {image_code}   — the character's portrait PATH from the NPC List (e.g. rudeus_greyrat/03_adult); the system builds the GitHub image URL (…/Images/<path>.jpg). A full image URL still works via the separate [CHAR|https://…|Name|#hex] URL header. REQUIRED: this header is ONLY for characters whose NPC List entry has a real image code. If their image is still the placeholder (xxxxxx.png) — or they have no image at all — use the imageless header [NPC|{Character Name}|{#hexcolor}] instead.
- {Character Name} — Canonical name as defined in the NPC List, INCLUDING the life-stage form where one exists (e.g. "Rudeus Greyrat (Teen)"). KEEP CONSISTENT regardless of user's language — names do not translate.
- {#hexcolor}    — Hex color from the NPC List (shared across all of a character's forms).

## IMAGELESS HEADER — [NPC|{Character Name}|{#hexcolor}]
For any character whose NPC List image is still the placeholder (xxxxxx.png), and for named characters with no image at all, use this header INSTEAD of [CHAR|...]. It takes no image code — the frame renders the character's initial as a glowing rune crest in their signature colour. It sits in the speech block exactly like the [CHAR] header.
Example: [NPC|Somal the Caravaneer|#8A8A5E]

## THE SPEECH BLOCK (HEADER SITS IN THE MIDDLE)
The header is the MIDDLE line of a strict 3-line block that is always used together:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (Monologue — only if the character is thinking)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            (this Header — or [NPC|Name|#hex] when the character has no image)
[SAY|Name|#hex|spoken words]           (Dialogue bubble)
- The three lines are CONTIGUOUS and in this exact order. The monologue (if any) sits directly ABOVE this header; the [SAY] bubble sits directly BELOW it.
- NEVER insert narration, action, or blank prose between [THINK] and [CHAR], or between [CHAR] and [SAY].
- Narration / action description for the character comes AFTER the [SAY] bubble (or before the whole block for scene-setting) — never between the tags.

## WHEN TO OUTPUT
Output [CHAR|…] immediately before any line of spoken dialogue, action description, or internal reaction attributed to a named character in the NPC List. One tag per character shift. Characters NOT in the list use the plain Name format from the Dialogues rule.

## CRITICAL ACCURACY RULE
The [CHAR] tag MUST match the character — AND the correct life-stage form — about to speak/act. A child Rudeus uses the (Child) form; the same scene must not mix his adult image. Image codes and hex colors are fixed in the NPC List — do not substitute.

## IMAGE BORDER BEHAVIOUR
The [CHAR] header always renders a framed avatar box (the "image border"); a working image code or direct URL fills the frame. If a code turns out broken, the frame falls back to the character's initial as a glowing rune monogram — but that is a safety net only. The CORRECT form for imageless characters is the [NPC|Name|#hex] header, which renders that same rune crest by design. Never fake a URL.

## EXAMPLE OUTPUT (EN)
[THINK|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3|This boy's mana pool is monstrous... I may have nothing left to teach him before long.]
[CHAR|roxy_migurdia/01_base|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3]
[SAY|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3|A mage who fears their own mana will never grow. Now — say the incantation with me, and mean it.]
She adjusted the wizard's hat that was a size too big and cleared her throat, trying to look authoritative.

[CHAR|rudeus_greyrat/01_child|Rudeus Greyrat (Child)|#6B4226]
[SAY|Rudeus Greyrat (Child)|#6B4226|...Like this, Roxy-sensei?]
The boy lifted his small hand, and water spiralled up without a single word of chant.

## EXAMPLE OUTPUT (TH)
[THINK|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3|พลังมานาของเด็กคนนี้มหาศาลเกินไป... อีกไม่นานฉันคงไม่เหลืออะไรจะสอนเขาแล้ว]
[CHAR|roxy_migurdia/01_base|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3]
[SAY|Roxy Migurdia|#3E6FA3|นักเวทที่กลัวมานาของตัวเองจะไม่มีวันเก่งขึ้น เอาล่ะ ท่องคาถาไปพร้อมฉัน และตั้งใจด้วย]
ร็อกซีจัดหมวกพ่อมดที่ใหญ่เกินตัวไปหนึ่งไซซ์ แล้วกระแอม พยายามทำท่าให้ดูน่าเชื่อถือ

[CHAR|rudeus_greyrat/01_child|Rudeus Greyrat (Child)|#6B4226]
[SAY|Rudeus Greyrat (Child)|#6B4226|...แบบนี้เหรอครับ ร็อกซี่อาจารย์?]
เด็กชายยกมือเล็กๆ ขึ้น แล้วสายน้ำก็พวยพุ่งเป็นเกลียวโดยไม่ต้องร่ายคาถาแม้แต่คำเดียว

## EXAMPLE OUTPUT (IMAGELESS NPC)
[NPC|Betty the Innkeeper|#B8860B]

Character names in the [CHAR|...] tag remain CANONICAL (English) regardless of roleplay language. Only spoken content and narration translate. The header UI is language-agnostic by design.

[DIALOGUE FORMAT RULE — ALWAYS FOLLOW]

ALL spoken dialogue is rendered with the Speech Bubble system (see "System — Dialogue / Speech Bubble [MT]"). NEVER write spoken lines as plain quoted "text".

Every time a named character speaks out loud, output the header then the bubble:

  [CHAR|{image}|{Character Name}|{#color}]
  [SAY|{Character Name}|{#color}|The spoken words here.]

Rules:
  1. The [CHAR|...] header above the bubble names the speaker — the bubble itself shows no name. Use the CANONICAL English name, including the life-stage form where one exists (e.g. "Eris Greyrat (Adult)").
  2. The [SAY] color MUST match the speaker's signature hex color from the NPC List ([CHAR] and [SAY] share the same color).
  3. Apply to EVERY speaking character — canon figures, side characters, adventurers, villagers, nobles, demons. {{user}}'s own lines are typed by the player.
  4. Internal thoughts use the Monologue system (italics) — NOT a bubble and NOT quotes.
  5. Narration and description stay as normal prose, separate from the bubbles.
  6. NEVER drop back to plain "Name: text" mid-scene. Every spoken line is a bubble under a header.
  7. LANGUAGE: spoken content matches {{user}}'s roleplay language; names always canonical English.
  8. The Name in [SAY|Name|#color|words] MUST be the speaker's canonical name INCLUDING life-stage form — identical to the [CHAR] header directly above. It prints as a small label on the bubble so the speaker stays clear even if the image or styling fails.

CORRECT example (EN):
  Rain ticked against the academy windows. Sylphiette set down her quill and looked up.
  [CHAR|sylphiette/03_adult|Sylphiette Greyrat (Adult)|#9FE0C8]
  [SAY|Sylphiette Greyrat (Adult)|#9FE0C8|You don't have to carry all of it alone, Rudeus. That's what I'm here for.]
  Rudeus's hand tightened around his staff.

The world of Mushoku Tensei is the Six-Faced World (Rokumen Sekai), so named because reality is rumoured to be shaped like a vast cube whose six faces are watched over by six gods. The peoples of the world live across its great landmasses and almost never grasp this true geometry. It is a world saturated with mana, populated by humans, demons, beasts, elves, dwarves, and rarer races, and quietly steered from the shadows by the Seven Great World Powers and the secret war of gods. Magic and the sword are the twin pillars of civilisation; the Adventurer's Guild threads the continents together; and history still bleeds from the Laplace Campaign four centuries past.

keys: ทวีปเซ็นทรัล
entries.content: The Central Continent is the largest and most populous landmass, the political heart of the world. It is home to the mighty Kingdom of Asura (the dominant human power) in the west, the twin Kingdoms of Shirone (North and South), the Magic Triumvirate and its Magic City of Sharia in the cold north-east, the Holy Land of the Sword where the Sword God Style is forged, and the Biheiril Kingdom near the eastern edge. Great mountain ranges and rivers carve it into regions, and travel between its kingdoms can take weeks or months by carriage and foot. It is the stage for most of the world's great politics and the recovery efforts after the Teleport Incident.

The Demon Continent is a harsh, mana-dense land of monster-infested wilds, broken roads, and scattered demon settlements and kingdoms. Its air is so thick with mana that magic comes easily but powerful monsters are everywhere, and its few teleportation circles are weak and unreliable. Many demon races make their home here, from the near-human to the monstrous, alongside the wandering courts of the immortal Demon Kings and the village of the blue-haired Migurd. It is here that the Teleport Incident strands the unlucky, forcing long and dangerous journeys to reach civilisation; towns such as Rikarisu and the harbour of Wind Port are key waypoints.

keys: ทวีปมิลลิส
entries.content: The Millis Continent is the seat of the Holy Country of Millis and the dominant Millis Church, a green and relatively orderly land west across the sea. Its grand holy capital, Millishion, anchors the faith, while the port of Westport handles trade and travellers arriving from the Demon Continent. The continent also holds the Great Forest, ancestral home of the proud Beast Race clans. The Millis faith preaches charity and order but carries an old prejudice against demons and demon-blooded peoples, a tension that colours much of continental life.

keys: ทวีปเบกาลิต
entries.content: The Begaritt Continent is a hot southern landmass of deserts, ruins, and brutal heat, sparsely settled and riddled with deep, deadly labyrinths. It is best known for the Great Labyrinth tied to the Teleport Incident, where treasure-hunters and rescue parties brave monsters and traps in its depths. Civilisation clings to oasis towns and the edges of the dungeons; deeper in, the land belongs to the monsters. It is the continent where the long search for the lost finally bottoms out.

keys: ปราสาทลอยฟ้า
ปราสาทบนฟ้า
ป้อมลอยฟ้า
ปราสาทเปรูกิอุส
entries.content: High above the world floats the Sky Castle, the fortress-home of the Armored Dragon King Perugius Dola, a hero of the Laplace Campaign. Within it he commands twelve summoned familiars, each a powerful spirit bound to his will, and conducts himself with proud ceremony toward any guest. The castle is one of the few sanctuaries truly beyond Hitogami's reach, and reaching it at all requires Perugius's summons. It is a place of ancient power, courtly ritual, and secrets from the war against Laplace.

keys: อาณาจักรอาสุระ
ราชอาณาจักรอาสุระ
entries.content: The Kingdom of Asura is the greatest and wealthiest human nation, occupying the western Central Continent and ruled from its grand Royal Capital. Its politics are a viper's nest of noble factions — among them the powerful Greyrat lineages of Notos, Boreas, Eris, and others — maneuvering around the throne. Asuran nobility prizes blood, name, and inheritance above almost all else, and a noble's word can outweigh a commoner's life. The Fittoa Region, where Rudeus is born, is one of its provinces.

keys: เขตฟิตโตอา

entries.content: The Fittoa Region is a rural province of the Kingdom of Asura, governed in part by the Boreas branch of the Greyrat nobility from the city of Roa. It is farmland, forest, and small villages — including Buena Village, Rudeus's birthplace. Quiet and ordinary, Fittoa becomes the epicentre of catastrophe when the Teleport Incident of Year 417 scatters nearly its entire population across the world in an instant, erasing the region as a living community and beginning the long age of search and reunion.

entries.content: The Kingdom of Shirone, divided into North and South Shirone, is a human realm of the Central Continent, smaller and more troubled than Asura. Its court intrigues and uneven rule produce both gifted and dangerous nobles — among them Prince Zanoba, whose monstrous strength and obsessive love of figurines make him an outcast among his own. Shirone's fortunes rise and fall with its neighbours and with the schemes that ripple out from the throne.

keys: สามอาณาจักรเวทมนตร์
อาณาจักรเวทมนตร์
entries.content: The Magic Triumvirate is an alliance of magic-focused kingdoms in the cold north-east of the Central Continent, centred on the Kingdom of Ranoa and the famed Magic City of Sharia. Here, magic is studied openly and prized above noble blood, making it one of the most tolerant places in the world for demons and mixed-bloods. The Ranoa University of Magic draws gifted students from every land. The Triumvirate's culture of learning and relative equality stands in sharp contrast to Asura's obsession with lineage and Millis's religious prejudice.

keys: ดินแดนศักดิ์สิทธิ์มิลลิส

entries.content: The Holy Country of Millis is the great religious nation of the Millis Continent, governed in close partnership with the Millis Church and its Pope. It preaches charity, order, and the worship of the god Millis, and its knightly orders and clergy wield real political power. Beneath the piety runs a deep prejudice: demons and the demon-blooded are widely distrusted or despised, and the Superd are reviled outright. Its holy capital is Millishion.

keys: อาณาจักรบิเฮริล
entries.content: The Biheiril Kingdom is a realm near the eastern reaches of the Central Continent, close to the Demon Continent's influence, with its own customs and a ruler of strong personality. It becomes significant in the later years of Rudeus's life as the hidden war widens and Orsted's network spreads. Like every court in this world, its politics conceal deeper currents — apostles, bargains, and the long reach of the gods.

keys: ดินแดนศักดิ์สิทธิ์แห่งดาบ

entries.comment: Place — Holy Land of the Sword
entries.content: The Holy Land of the Sword is the spiritual and training home of the Sword God Style, set in the harsh northern Central Continent. Here the Sword God himself, Gal Farion, presides, and disciples are drilled until the draw-and-strike of the style is faster than thought. Children are sent here to be forged into living blades; Eris trains here to rise to Sword King. It is a austere, martial place where strength is the only currency and mercy is rare.

Roa is the principal city of the Fittoa Region and the seat of the Boreas branch of House Greyrat. It is here, in the Boreas mansion, that a young Rudeus is taken on as tutor to the wild noble girl Eris, and where Ghislaine trains her sword. Roa is a comfortable provincial city of nobles, servants, and merchants — until the Teleport Incident tears it and the whole region apart in an instant.

keys: หมู่บ้านบูเอนา
entries.content: Buena Village is the small rural village in the Fittoa Region where Rudeus Greyrat is born and spends his earliest childhood. It is here he is raised by Paul and Zenith, taught magic by Roxy, and first befriends the timid, green-haired Sylphiette. An ordinary place of fields and neighbours, it represents the warm, quiet beginning of Rudeus's second life before the wider world and its dangers reach him.

keys: เมืองเวทมนตร์
นครเวทมนตร์
entries.content: The Magic City of Sharia is the jewel of the Kingdom of Ranoa and the Magic Triumvirate — a cold, snow-dusted city built around the study and practice of magic. It is famously tolerant: demons, beastfolk, elves, and mixed-bloods walk its streets with far less fear than elsewhere. The city is dominated by the Ranoa University of Magic, and Rudeus makes his adult home here, raising a family and building his base as the hidden war deepens. It is a hub of research, adventurers, and quiet intrigue.

keys: มหาวิทยาลัยรานัว

entries.content: The Ranoa University of Magic in Sharia is the most prestigious magical institution in the world, drawing gifted and noble students of every race. It teaches the schools of magic, hosts research, and is run with a student council and a famously eccentric, powerful staff. Here Rudeus reunites with Sylphiette (disguised as the bodyguard "Fitz"), befriends Cliff, Zanoba, Linia and Pursena, and takes Julie and others as disciples. Its halls mix learning, rivalry, romance, and the first threads of larger conspiracies.

Rikarisu is a town on the Demon Continent and one of the first points of refuge for those flung there by the Teleport Incident. A rough frontier settlement surrounded by monster-haunted wilds, it is where stranded travellers take guild work, gather supplies, and begin the long journey toward the coast and civilisation. For Rudeus, Eris, and Ruijerd it is an early waypoint on the road that earns them the name "Dead End".

keys: วินด์พอร์ต

entries.content: Wind Port is a major harbour town on the coast of the Demon Continent, the gateway for crossing the sea toward the Millis Continent. A bustle of sailors, merchants, adventurers, and demon-folk, it is where long overland journeys finally meet the water. Passage is expensive and the docks are full of opportunists, making it both a relief and a test for travellers who have crossed the Demon Continent's dangers to reach it.

keys: เวสต์พอร์ต

entries.content: Westport is the great port city on the Millis Continent where travellers from the Demon Continent first set foot in the lands of Millis. It is orderly and prosperous compared to the demon lands, but newcomers — especially the demon-blooded — quickly feel the weight of Millis prejudice. From Westport, roads lead inland toward the holy capital and the Great Forest.

keys: มิลลิชิออน
เมืองหลวงศักดิ์สิทธิ์
entries.content: Millishion is the holy capital of the Holy Country of Millis and the seat of the Millis Church and its Pope. It is a grand, devout city of cathedrals, clergy, knightly orders, and pilgrims, the beating heart of the world's dominant faith. Charity and order are its public virtues; behind them sit church politics, wealth, and an entrenched suspicion of demons. It is the home turf of figures like Cliff Grimoire's powerful grandfather, the Pope.

keys: ป่าศักดิ์สิทธิ์
entries.content: The Great Forest is a vast, ancient woodland on the Millis Continent, the ancestral homeland of the proud Beast Race. Its clans live by strength, honour, and keen animal senses, governing themselves apart from the human kingdoms. Deep within dwells the Great Holy Beast, a revered sacred creature. Outsiders are met with wary suspicion, and respect must be earned through strength, not words.

keys: หมู่บ้านโดลเดีย
entries.content: Doldia Village is the home settlement of the Dedoldia and Adoldia clans deep in the Great Forest, governed by the Beast Race's warrior customs. It is the birthplace of the swordswoman Ghislaine and of the noblewomen Linia and Pursena. Strength and lineage decide status among its people, and the village guards its traditions and its sacred Holy Beast fiercely. Outsiders who arrive without strength or a worthy purpose are not welcome.

keys: หมู่บ้านมิเกิร์ด
entries.content: The Migurd Village, set in the wilds of the Demon Continent, is the home of the blue-haired, child-faced Migurd race — a gentle demon people who communicate by telepathy and age extraordinarily slowly. It is the birthplace of Roxy Migurdia, who left it to see the world and became a wandering mage. Peaceful and insular, the village shelters its people from a world that often fears or underestimates demons.

keys: เมืองหลวงอาสุระ
entries.content: The Royal Capital of the Kingdom of Asura is the seat of the throne and the centre of human political power in the world. It is a city of palaces, noble estates, and ceaseless court intrigue, where the great Greyrat factions and other houses scheme around the crown. A commoner can be made or destroyed by a noble's whim here, and the deeper one rises toward the throne, the more dangerous the game becomes.

keys: เขาวงกตวาร์ป
เขาวงกตใหญ่
entries.content: The Begaritt Continent holds the world's most dangerous labyrinths — sprawling underground dungeons of monsters, traps, and treasure that swallow careless adventurers whole. The Teleport Labyrinth in particular is bound up with the Mana Calamity, and it is in its depths that the long search for those lost in the Teleport Incident reaches its grim climax. Labyrinth-diving is deadly, lucrative work: parties balance greed against survival floor by floor, and many never return.

keys: พลังเวทมนตร์
ระบบเวทมนตร์
entries.content: Magic is powered by mana (magic power), an internal reserve that every caster draws upon to shape spells. A person's maximum mana is largely innate, but it can be brutally expanded in childhood by repeatedly exhausting it to near-empty and letting it regrow larger — a punishing method that produces monsters of capacity like Rudeus. Spells are normally shaped by incantation (chanting), with longer chants for stronger effects, though prodigies learn to cast silently. Mana recovers with rest; run dry and a caster is helpless until it returns.

Attack Magic is the elemental school, divided into Water, Fire, Wind, and Earth, each scaling from small utility tricks to devastating large-scale destruction at higher tiers. Water includes ice and healing-adjacent control; Fire brings raw heat and explosion; Wind grants blades, gusts, and mobility; Earth raises walls, stone, and crushing mass. A versatile mage learns several elements, and the strongest can reshape battlefields and terrain. Rudeus's mastery of silent, high-tier elemental magic — especially Water and Earth — is a cornerstone of his power.

## SPELL COMPENDIUM & CHANTS (EN / TH)
Tier ladder: Elementary → Intermediate → Advanced → Saint → King → Emperor → God. The verses below are this story's canonical chants — speak the verse, finish with the spell's name, and the casting completes. Chant-less casters (Rudeus, and true masters) may skip the verse and pay only focus and mana. Combined magic (Ice from refined Water; Lightning from Water × Wind) demands greater control and a higher tier than its parts.

### WATER MAGIC (เวทน้ำ)
1. Water Ball — Elementary / วอเตอร์บอล — hurls a fist-sized sphere of compressed water; the first attack spell most mages ever learn.
   Chant EN: "O great spirits of water, heed my call and loose thy pure stream — Water Ball!"
   คาถา TH: "โอ้ภูตแห่งวารีผู้ยิ่งใหญ่ จงสดับเสียงข้า ปลดปล่อยสายธารอันบริสุทธิ์เถิด — วอเตอร์บอล!"
2. Water Cannon — Intermediate / วอเตอร์แคนนอน — a pressurised lance of water that punches through shields and plate armor.
   Chant EN: "Gather, torrent of the deep, and strike as the spear of the river god — Water Cannon!"
   คาถา TH: "จงรวมกันเถิด กระแสธารแห่งห้วงลึก พุ่งทะยานดั่งหอกแห่งเทพนที — วอเตอร์แคนนอน!"
3. Waterfall — Advanced / วอเตอร์ฟอลล์ — calls a crushing cascade down along a wide line, sweeping whole formations off their feet.
   Chant EN: "Rise, river of heaven, and fall upon mine enemies as the wrath of the flood — Waterfall!"
   คาถา TH: "จงทะยานขึ้นเถิด สายนทีแห่งสรวงสวรรค์ แล้วถาโถมลงเหนือศัตรูดั่งพิโรธแห่งอุทกภัย — วอเตอร์ฟอลล์!"
4. Cumulonimbus — Saint / คิวมูโลนิมบัส — seeds a true stormfront over an entire district: hours of rain, hail and gale. The benchmark spell of a Water Saint; Roxy's proof of rank.
   Chant EN: "O king of clouds, dark anvil of the sky, gather above and open thy hundred gates of rain — Cumulonimbus!"
   คาถา TH: "โอ้ราชันแห่งเมฆา ทั่งดำแห่งนภากาศ จงรวมตัวเบื้องบนแลเปิดประตูสายฝนร้อยบานเถิด — คิวมูโลนิมบัส!"
5. Maelstrom of the Abyss — King / มหาวังวนแห่งห้วงลึก — floods the battlefield and drags it into a spinning deluge; a siege-breaking, army-drowning working.
   Chant EN: "Sea without shore, hunger without end, swallow the earth and grind it in thy turning throat — Maelstrom!"
   คาถา TH: "ทะเลอันไร้ฝั่ง ความหิวโหยอันไร้สิ้นสุด จงกลืนกินแผ่นดินแลบดขยี้ในเกลียววังวนแห่งเจ้า — มหาวังวน!"

### ICE MAGIC (เวทน้ำแข็ง — สายผสมของน้ำ)
1. Icicle Edge — Intermediate / ไอซิเคิลเอดจ์ — fires razor spears of ice; Rudeus's favoured silent quick-kill bolt.
   Chant EN: "Freeze, breath of winter, and fly as fangs of the frozen wolf — Icicle Edge!"
   คาถา TH: "จงเยือกแข็งเถิด ลมหายใจแห่งเหมันต์ โบยบินดั่งเขี้ยวหมาป่าน้ำแข็ง — ไอซิเคิลเอดจ์!"
2. Frost Bind — Intermediate / ฟรอสต์ไบนด์ — freezes feet, joints and sword-hands, locking a foe where they stand.
   Chant EN: "Creep and cling, rime of the still lake, and hold my foe in thy white grasp — Frost Bind!"
   คาถา TH: "จงคืบคลานแลเกาะกุม เกล็ดน้ำแข็งแห่งทะเลสาบนิ่งงัน ตรึงศัตรูไว้ในเงื้อมมือขาวโพลน — ฟรอสต์ไบนด์!"
3. Icicle Field — Advanced / ไอซิเคิลฟิลด์ — erupts a forest of ice lances from the ground across a wide area.
   Chant EN: "Sleep no more, buried winter — rise a thousand spears and make this field thy garden — Icicle Field!"
   คาถา TH: "จงตื่นเถิดเหมันต์ใต้พสุธา ผงาดขึ้นเป็นหอกพันเล่ม เนรมิตสมรภูมิให้เป็นอุทยานน้ำแข็ง — ไอซิเคิลฟิลด์!"
4. Glacial Tomb — Saint / สุสานธารน้ำแข็ง — entombs everything within a zone in grave-deep ice.
   Chant EN: "Silence of the deep glacier, close thy jaws and keep what I give thee until the thaw of ages — Glacial Tomb!"
   คาถา TH: "ความเงียบงันแห่งธารน้ำแข็งลึกล้ำ จงงับขากรรไกรลง เก็บสิ่งที่ข้ามอบไว้จนกว่ายุคสมัยจะละลาย — สุสานธารน้ำแข็ง!"
5. Eternal Winter — King / เหมันต์นิรันดร์ — locks an entire region beneath blizzard and killing frost for days.
   Chant EN: "Come, white queen of the ending year — lay thy pale kingdom over all this land — Eternal Winter!"
   คาถา TH: "เสด็จมาเถิด ราชินีขาวแห่งปลายปี จงแผ่ราชอาณาจักรซีดขาวของพระองค์คลุมแผ่นดินนี้ทั้งมวล — เหมันต์นิรันดร์!"

### FIRE MAGIC (เวทไฟ)
1. Fireball — Elementary / ไฟร์บอล — a bursting orb of flame; simple, loud, effective.
   Chant EN: "Kindle and roar, ember of the first hearth, and burst upon my foe — Fireball!"
   คาถา TH: "จงลุกโชนแลคำราม ถ่านไฟแห่งเตาแรกเริ่ม ระเบิดใส่ศัตรูของข้าเถิด — ไฟร์บอล!"
2. Flame Lance — Intermediate / หอกเพลิง — a piercing spear of white-hot fire that stabs through the line.
   Chant EN: "Straighten, tongue of the forge, into a spear no shield may cool — Flame Lance!"
   คาถา TH: "จงเหยียดตรงเถิด เปลวลิ้นแห่งเตาหลอม แปรเป็นหอกที่ไร้โล่ใดดับได้ — หอกเพลิง!"
3. Firestorm — Advanced / พายุเพลิง — a rolling wave of flame that scours a swath of the field.
   Chant EN: "Dance wide, red harvest wind, and reap all that stands in thy burning row — Firestorm!"
   คาถา TH: "จงร่ายรำให้กว้างเถิด วาตะเก็บเกี่ยวสีแดง เผาผลาญทุกสิ่งที่ยืนขวางแนวไฟแห่งเจ้า — พายุเพลิง!"
4. Sea of Flames — Saint / ทะเลเพลิง — ignites the ground itself into a burning tide.
   Chant EN: "Open, furnace under the world — let thy molten sea rise and drown the field in light — Sea of Flames!"
   คาถา TH: "จงเปิดออกเถิด เตาหลอมใต้พิภพ ให้ทะเลหลอมเหลวเอ่อขึ้นท่วมสมรภูมิด้วยแสงเพลิง — ทะเลเพลิง!"
5. Purgatory Pillar — King / เสาเพลิงชำระ — a column of fire that melts stone and unmakes fortifications.
   Chant EN: "Judgement of the burning heaven, descend as one pillar and leave nothing unpurified — Purgatory!"
   คาถา TH: "คำพิพากษาแห่งสวรรค์เพลิง จงเสด็จลงเป็นเสาหนึ่งเดียว มิให้สิ่งใดรอดพ้นการชำระ — เพอร์กาทอรี!"

### WIND MAGIC (เวทลม)
1. Wind Cutter — Elementary / ใบมีดวายุ — an invisible blade of compressed air.
   Chant EN: "Whet thyself, unseen edge of the sky, and cut where I bid — Wind Cutter!"
   คาถา TH: "จงลับคมเถิด คมดาบล่องหนแห่งนภา แล้วเชือดเฉือน ณ ที่ข้าชี้ — ใบมีดวายุ!"
2. Sonic Boom — Intermediate / โซนิคบูม — a concussive burst of air that shatters, deafens and hurls men from their feet.
   Chant EN: "Clap, hands of the storm giant, and break the air upon mine enemies — Sonic Boom!"
   คาถา TH: "จงตบสนั่นเถิด ฝ่ามือแห่งยักษ์พายุ ทุบอากาศให้แตกใส่เหล่าศัตรู — โซนิคบูม!"
3. Tempest Claw — Advanced / กรงเล็บพายุ — raking shear-winds that flay armor and shred banners.
   Chant EN: "Stoop from thy height, grey falcon of the gale, and rake the earth with five talons — Tempest Claw!"
   คาถา TH: "จงโฉบลงจากเบื้องสูงเถิด เหยี่ยวเทาแห่งวายุ ตะครุบพสุธาด้วยกรงเล็บทั้งห้า — กรงเล็บพายุ!"
4. Sky Rend — Saint / ฉีกนภา — a downburst that flattens everything beneath a chosen stretch of sky.
   Chant EN: "Break, roof of the world — fall as one breath and press all beneath thee flat — Sky Rend!"
   คาถา TH: "จงแตกสลายเถิด หลังคาแห่งโลกา ถล่มลงเป็นลมหายใจเดียว บดทุกสิ่งเบื้องล่างให้ราบ — ฉีกนภา!"
5. Heaven's Howl — King / คำรามสวรรค์ — a walking hurricane bound to the caster's will.
   Chant EN: "Wake, oldest wind that circled the first sea — walk with me and howl my enemies to dust — Heaven's Howl!"
   คาถา TH: "จงตื่นเถิด วาตะเฒ่าผู้เวียนรอบทะเลแรก จงย่างเดินเคียงข้า แลคำรามให้ศัตรูแหลกเป็นธุลี — คำรามสวรรค์!"

### EARTH MAGIC (เวทดิน)
1. Stone Cannon — Elementary (scales with mana) / สโตนแคนนอน — Rudeus's signature: a dense stone shot; fed enough mana it strikes like a ballista bolt.
   Chant EN: "Harden, bone of the mountain, and fly true from my hand — Stone Cannon!"
   คาถา TH: "จงแกร่งเถิด อัฐิแห่งขุนเขา แล้วพุ่งตรงจากหัตถ์ข้า — สโตนแคนนอน!"
2. Earth Wall — Elementary / กำแพงปฐพี — raises a rampart of packed earth and stone.
   Chant EN: "Rise and shield, patient soil, as the rampart that never marches — Earth Wall!"
   คาถา TH: "จงผงาดขึ้นปกป้องเถิด ปฐพีผู้อดทน ดั่งปราการที่ไม่เคยเคลื่อนทัพ — กำแพงปฐพี!"
3. Quagmire — Intermediate / ปลักตม — turns the ground into clinging swamp; the mire Rudeus used against even Orsted.
   Chant EN: "Soften and swallow, hungry field, and hold every heel that trusts thee — Quagmire!"
   คาถา TH: "จงอ่อนเหลวแลกลืนกินเถิด ท้องทุ่งผู้หิวโหย ยึดทุกส้นเท้าที่หลงวางใจเจ้า — ปลักตม!"
4. Earth Fortress — Advanced / ป้อมปฐพี — raises walls, towers and bunkers from the living ground in moments.
   Chant EN: "Remember thy old shape, sleeping stone, and stand once more as castle and keep — Earth Fortress!"
   คาถา TH: "จงหวนระลึกถึงรูปทรงเก่าก่อนเถิด ศิลาที่หลับใหล ผงาดขึ้นเป็นปราสาทแลหอคอยอีกครา — ป้อมปฐพี!"
5. Tectonic Rift — Saint / รอยแยกธรณี — tears the battlefield open along a fault of the caster's choosing.
   Chant EN: "Groan and part, spine of the world — open thy black mouth beneath my foes — Tectonic Rift!"
   คาถา TH: "จงครวญครางแลแยกออกเถิด สันหลังแห่งโลกา เปิดปากมืดมิดของเจ้าใต้เหล่าศัตรู — รอยแยกธรณี!"

### LIGHTNING MAGIC (เวทสายฟ้า — สายผสม น้ำ × ลม)
1. Spark — Intermediate / ประกายฟ้า — a snapping arc that staggers and numbs.
   Chant EN: "Leap, little serpent of the storm, and bite the hand raised against me — Spark!"
   คาถา TH: "จงกระโจนเถิด อสรพิษน้อยแห่งพายุ ฉกกัดมือที่เงื้อใส่ข้า — ประกายฟ้า!"
2. Thunderline — Advanced / สายอสุนี — a bolt that chains through ranks of foes in a jagged line.
   Chant EN: "Run thy white road, herald of thunder, from breast to breast until none still stand — Thunderline!"
   คาถา TH: "จงวิ่งไปตามถนนขาวของเจ้าเถิด ผู้ประกาศแห่งอสุนีบาต จากอกสู่อกจนไม่มีผู้ใดยืนอยู่ — สายอสุนี!"
3. Lightning — Saint / ไลท์นิ่ง — calls a true bolt down from a summoned cloud; sky-to-earth judgement.
   Chant EN: "Gather, cloud; sharpen, light — and fall as the one spear no armor has ever turned — Lightning!"
   คาถา TH: "จงรวมตัวเถิดเมฆา จงลับคมเถิดแสงสว่าง แล้วตกลงมาเป็นหอกเดียวที่ไร้เกราะใดต้านทาน — ไลท์นิ่ง!"
4. Wrath of the Storm God — King / พิโรธเทพพายุ — a sustained bombardment of bolts across the field.
   Chant EN: "Throne of thunder, tip thy quiver — let every arrow of the sky remember my enemy's name — Wrath of the Storm God!"
   คาถา TH: "บัลลังก์อสุนี จงเทแล่งธนูของพระองค์ ให้ศรทุกดอกแห่งนภาจดจำนามศัตรูของข้า — พิโรธเทพพายุ!"

keys: เวทเยียวยา
entries.content:
Healing Magic mends wounds, knits flesh, and restores the injured, and its practitioners are valued everywhere — especially within the Millis Church, where healers are often clergy. It has hard limits: it cannot raise the dead, cannot regrow what has been wholly and permanently lost, and cannot cure poison (that is Detoxification's domain). Higher tiers heal faster and graver wounds, but the cost in mana and skill rises steeply. Zenith Greyrat is a trained healer of this school.

## SPELL LIST & CHANTS (EN / TH)
1. Healing — Elementary / ฮีลลิ่ง — closes cuts, stops bleeding, dulls pain.
   Chant EN: "Warm hand of mercy, close what was opened and still the red river — Healing!"
   คาถา TH: "หัตถ์อุ่นแห่งเมตตา จงปิดสิ่งที่ถูกเปิด แลหยุดสายธารสีแดงเถิด — ฮีลลิ่ง!"
2. Ex Healing — Advanced / เอ็กซ์ฮีลลิ่ง — knits deep wounds and broken bone; can rejoin a freshly severed limb if applied at once.
   Chant EN: "Weaver of flesh, take up thy shuttle and mend this body to the pattern it remembers — Ex Healing!"
   คาถา TH: "ผู้ถักทอเรือนกาย จงหยิบกระสวยของเจ้า ซ่อมสรีระนี้ให้คืนสู่แบบแผนที่มันจดจำ — เอ็กซ์ฮีลลิ่ง!"
3. Shine Healing — Saint / ไชน์ฮีลลิ่ง — bathes the gravely wounded in restoring light; drags the dying back from the threshold.
   Chant EN: "Light that stood at the first dawn, pour through me and forbid this soul the dark door — Shine Healing!"
   คาถา TH: "แสงที่ยืนอยู่ ณ อรุณรุ่งแรก จงหลั่งไหลผ่านข้า แลห้ามดวงวิญญาณนี้ผ่านประตูมืด — ไชน์ฮีลลิ่ง!"
LIMITS (unchanged, binding): no resurrection; nothing long-lost regrows; poison and disease belong to Detoxification.

Detoxification Magic neutralises poisons, venoms, diseases, and toxins that ordinary Healing Magic cannot touch. It is a specialised and less common discipline, prized by adventurers who face venomous monsters and by those wary of assassination. A skilled detoxifier can save a life that a healer cannot, making the two schools complementary rather than interchangeable.

## SPELL LIST & CHANTS (EN / TH)
1. Detoxification — Elementary / ดีท็อกซิฟิเคชัน — neutralises common venoms and tainted food or water.
   Chant EN: "Clear spring beneath the skin, rise and carry the serpent's gift away — Detoxification!"
   คาถา TH: "ธารน้ำใสใต้ผิวกาย จงผุดขึ้นแลพัดพาของขวัญแห่งอสรพิษออกไป — ดีท็อกซิฟิเคชัน!"
2. Ex Detoxification — Advanced / เอ็กซ์ดีท็อกซิฟิเคชัน — purges rare toxins, plagues and monster venoms that resist lesser casting.
   Chant EN: "Deep well of white water, wash bone and blood and leave no root of rot behind — Ex Detoxification!"
   คาถา TH: "บ่อน้ำขาวลึกล้ำ จงชะล้างกระดูกแลโลหิต มิให้รากแห่งความเน่าเปื่อยหลงเหลือ — เอ็กซ์ดีท็อกซิฟิเคชัน!"
3. Sacred Purification — Saint / มหาชำระศักดิ์สิทธิ์ — cleanses a whole body — or a whole well, ward or camp — of poison, plague and creeping blight at once.
   Chant EN: "First rain that ever fell clean, fall again through all this place and make it as it was — Sacred Purification!"
   คาถา TH: "ฝนแรกที่เคยตกลงมาบริสุทธิ์ จงโปรยปรายผ่านสถานที่นี้อีกครา คืนทุกสิ่งสู่ดังเดิม — มหาชำระศักดิ์สิทธิ์!"

keys: เวทอัญเชิญ
entries.content:
Summoning Magic calls forth spirits, familiars, and creatures to fight or serve at the caster's command. It is rare and difficult, demanding both knowledge and the means to bind what is summoned. Its grandest expression is seen in the Armored Dragon King Perugius, who commands twelve mighty summoned familiars from his Sky Castle. In most hands it is far more modest, but a capable summoner can field allies no lone caster could match.

## SPELL LIST & CHANTS (EN / TH)
1. Spirit Call — Elementary / เรียกภูต — coaxes a minor spirit (light, breeze, ember) to scout, glow or carry a whisper.
   Chant EN: "Small neighbour of the other side, step through and lend me thy little hand — Spirit Call!"
   คาถา TH: "เพื่อนบ้านตัวน้อยแห่งฟากโพ้น จงก้าวผ่านมาแลยื่นมือน้อยของเจ้าให้ข้า — เรียกภูต!"
2. Familiar Pact — Intermediate / พันธสัญญาสัตว์อารักษ์ — binds a beast or spirit as a lasting familiar; it shares senses and simple commands.
   Chant EN: "By my name given and thy name kept, walk beside me until one of us falls — Familiar Pact!"
   คาถา TH: "ด้วยนามที่ข้ามอบแลนามที่เจ้าเก็บงำ จงเดินเคียงข้าจนกว่าฝ่ายหนึ่งจะล้มลง — พันธสัญญาสัตว์อารักษ์!"
3. Guardian Gate — Advanced / ประตูผู้พิทักษ์ — opens a short-lived gate and calls a battle-grade entity to fight at the caster's side.
   Chant EN: "Hinge of the hidden door, turn for me — send out thy keeper armed and willing — Guardian Gate!"
   คาถา TH: "บานพับแห่งประตูเร้นลับ จงหมุนเปิดเพื่อข้า ส่งผู้เฝ้าประตูพร้อมอาวุธแลใจสมัครออกมา — ประตูผู้พิทักษ์!"
4. Grand Summons — Emperor / มหาอัญเชิญ — the discipline's summit: binding great named familiars in lasting service, as Perugius binds his Twelve at the Sky Castle.
   Chant EN: "Thrones of the far court, hear the debt I offer and the crown I wager — attend me, named and mighty — Grand Summons!"
   คาถา TH: "บัลลังก์ทั้งหลายแห่งราชสำนักไกลโพ้น จงฟังหนี้ที่ข้าเสนอแลมงกุฎที่ข้าวางเดิมพัน มาปรากฏเถิด ผู้ทรงนามแลทรงพลัง — มหาอัญเชิญ!"

Cursed Magic deals in curses — lingering, often cruel magical afflictions laid upon a person or thing. Curses can warp the body, compel behaviour, or doom a bloodline, and they are notoriously hard to remove. Elinalise Dragonroad lives under such a curse, which drives a relentless appetite she must satisfy or sicken. Feared and poorly understood, cursed magic sits at the dark edge of magical study.

keys: เวทไร้รูปแบบ
entries.content: Formless Magic is a rare and subtle school that does not follow the fixed shapes of the elemental or healing disciplines, instead manipulating mana directly in flexible, unconventional ways — reinforcement, manipulation, and effects that defy ordinary classification. Because it lacks set incantations, it is difficult to teach and rewards intuition and raw control. It is part of what makes the most creative mages dangerous beyond their tier.

entries.content: Magical skill is graded in ascending tiers: Novice (Elementary), Intermediate, Advanced, Saint, King, Emperor, and the legendary God tier. Each step up represents a vast leap in power, range, and the difficulty of the spells one can wield; most career mages plateau at Intermediate or Advanced. Saint-tier (like Roxy's water magic) marks a true expert; King and Emperor are nation-class talents; God-tier is the stuff of legend. A mage's rank is often certified and recognised across kingdoms and the Guild.

entries.content: Chant-less (silent) casting is the ability to invoke magic without speaking the incantation, casting in an instant and giving an enemy no warning. It is the unmistakable mark of a prodigy — most mages, even strong ones, cannot do it and remain bound to vocal chants whose length grows with a spell's power. Rudeus's silent, rapid-fire high-tier magic is one of his deadliest advantages, letting him out-tempo casters who must chant. Mastering it requires extraordinary control and an internalised feel for the spell.

keys: วงเวทเทเลพอร์ต
entries.content: Teleportation in this world is achieved through magic circles — inscribed arrays that move people and things across distance. The technology is rare, expensive, and dangerous: circles can be unstable, short-ranged, or wildly inaccurate, and the Demon Continent's circles are especially unreliable. The catastrophic misuse and overload of such magic lies behind the Teleport Incident of Year 417. Reliable long-range teleportation is a closely guarded, world-changing power.

keys: เครื่องมือเวท
สิ่งประดิษฐ์เวท
entries.content: Magic Items and tools store or channel magic for those who cannot cast freely, ranging from everyday conveniences (light-stones, heated tools) to powerful relics and enchanted armour. The greatest are the Magic Armors and ancient artifacts left from past ages, capable of feats no ordinary craft could match. Researchers like Cliff Grimoire and crafters like Zanoba and Rudeus study and create such items, and a single legendary artifact can shift the balance of a battle or a kingdom.

entries.content:
The Sword God Style (Kenshin-ryū) is the most aggressive of the three great sword schools, built on the philosophy of striking first and striking hardest — the fastest draw and the heaviest single blow. Its masters can cut faster than the eye can follow, ending fights in one decisive strike. Forged in the Holy Land of the Sword under the Sword God Gal Farion, it produces ferocious duelists like Eris and Ghislaine. Its ranks climb from practitioner to Sword Saint, Sword King, Sword Emperor, and finally Sword God.

## MOVE LIST — SWORD GOD STYLE (7 named techniques, EN / TH)
The school counts seven named techniques; ranks unlock them roughly in order, and the seventh is the secret art reserved for Sword King and above.
1. First Fang / เขี้ยวแรก — the opening doctrine made flesh: a single committed cut thrown before the enemy settles; most duels end here.
2. Twin Falcons / เหยี่ยวคู่ — two strikes in one motion — the second falcon dives exactly where the dodge must go.
3. Stone Splitter / ผ่าศิลา — a full-body vertical blow that bursts guards, shields and breastplates alike.
4. Flash Draw / วาดดาบสายฟ้าแลบ — an explosive draw-cut from the sheath that closes distance in a blink; the duelist's answer to archers and mages.
5. Silver Gale / วายุเงิน — a passing run through the enemy's line, one cut per step, none wasted.
6. Sky Sunder / ฟาดฟ้า — a leaping overhead finisher that trades everything for one mountain-splitting impact.
7. Longsword of Light / ดาบยาวแห่งแสง (Hikari no Tachi) — SECRET ART. The legendary light-speed draw of the Sword God Style: the blade arrives with the flash, before thought, before sound. Gal Farion's proof of godhood; Eris and Ghislaine both chase its perfection.

keys: สำนักเทพน้ำ
วิชาเทพน้ำ
entries.content:
The Water God Style (Suijin-ryū) is the defensive counterpart to the Sword God Style, founded on flowing defense, redirection, and turning an opponent's force back against them. Where Sword God strikes first, Water God waits, parries, and counters — the art of survival, patience, and reading an enemy. Paul Greyrat trained in this style alongside others. Its highest masters can deflect almost any attack and win by outlasting rather than overpowering.

## MOVE LIST — WATER GOD STYLE (7 named techniques, EN / TH)
The school counts seven named techniques; all grow from the first principle — receive, redirect, return.
1. Flow (Nagashi) / นากาชิ — สายน้ำไหล — the root of the style: a soft parry that lets the enemy's force slide harmlessly past its target.
2. Still Water Stance / ท่าน้ำนิ่ง — the waiting guard; utterly calm, offering nothing, reading everything.
3. Falling Leaf / ใบไม้ร่วง — receives even a two-handed blow and guides it gently into the ground.
4. Whirlpool / น้ำวน — a circular bind that wrenches the weapon from the attacker's grip mid-swing.
5. Riptide / กระแสย้อน — the instant counter: the parry and the answering thrust are one motion.
6. Mirror Surface / ผิวน้ำกระจก — the mind-art: reflecting feints back at the feinter, striking the lie instead of the blade.
7. Torrent Reversal / มหาวารีย้อนกลับ — SECRET ART. The perfected counter of the Water God Style: the enemy's own killing blow is received whole and returned doubled — the harder they strike, the harder they fall.

keys: สำนักเทพเหนือ
วิชาเทพเหนือ
entries.content:
The North God Style (Hokushin-ryū) is the pragmatic, adaptable school — "win by any means." It weaponises terrain, feints, hidden tools, dirty tricks, and unorthodox tactics rather than pure form, making its fighters unpredictable and hard to counter. It blends well with magic and improvisation, valuing victory over honour. Its ranks rise to North King, North Emperor, and North God; the reigning North God, Kalman, sits among the Seven Great World Powers.

## MOVE LIST — NORTH GOD STYLE (6 named tricks, EN / TH)
The school refuses fixed kata — "win by any means" is its whole scripture — but six named tricks are passed from master to student.
1. Anything Goes / ชนะได้ทุกวิถีทาง — the founding doctrine counted as the first technique: sand in the eyes, a thrown boot, a hidden second knife — victory is the only form.
2. Crow Feint / กาลวงตา — a layered feint that sells one lie to hide a second; even Water God readers bite on the wrong layer.
3. Chained Fang / เขี้ยวโซ่ตรวน — fighting with chain, rope-dart or weighted cord; binds the blade, then the throat.
4. Dead Angle / มุมมรณะ — the art of standing where the enemy cannot look: blind spots, low sun, narrow stairs, a hostage's shadow.
5. Beast Lunge / ตะครุบสัตว์ร้าย — an animal takedown — shoulder to hip, both to the ground, dagger ending the argument.
6. Trickster's Finale / ฉากสุดท้ายนักลวง — SECRET ART. The feigned defeat: dropped guard, stumbled knee, begged mercy — and the lethal reversal the moment the enemy believes it.

entries.content: Martial mastery in the sword schools is graded much like magic, in ascending tiers: from trained practitioner up through Saint, King, Emperor, and God of the respective style (Sword, Water, or North). Each tier marks a profound leap — a Sword King can carve through ordinary soldiers effortlessly, while an Emperor or God is a one-person army. These titles are recognised across the world and earn enormous respect or fear. Eris reaches Sword King; Ghislaine is a Sword King; the style Gods rank among the mightiest beings alive.
